---
title: Jira (PSOM)
---

Metrics in this section are calculated from PSOM Jira Service Management ticket
data.

The relevant Jira table is:

- [`jira.psom`](../catalog/jira.qmd#psom) - weekly snapshot of all PSOM tickets

**Data cadence.** The `jira.psom` table is updated once per week (every Monday
at ~06:00 ET). Each run writes a full snapshot of all current tickets to a new
`date=` partition; previous partitions are preserved as an audit trail. Always
filter to `date = (SELECT MAX(date) FROM jira.psom)` when querying the latest
state.

## PSOM Metrics

{{< include jira/tickets-by-status.qmd >}}

{{< include jira/weekly-ticket-volume.qmd >}}

{{< include jira/tickets-by-priority.qmd >}}

<!-- Tickets by Request Type is held back: request_type is empty for every -->
<!-- ticket in the latest snapshot, so all rows collapse into a single -->
<!-- (non-service-request) bucket. Likely a psom-weekly-sync extraction gap -->
<!-- rather than a genuine absence. Re-enable this include once request_type -->
<!-- is populated upstream. -->
<!-- {{< include jira/tickets-by-request-type.qmd >}} -->

<!-- The two SLA breach-rate metrics are held back until the psom-weekly-sync -->
<!-- pipeline populates the sla_first_response_breached / sla_time_to_done_breached -->
<!-- columns. As of the latest snapshot those columns are empty for every ticket -->
<!-- (only the sla_*_due timestamps are captured), so both metrics return an empty -->
<!-- result. The fragments are kept in the repo; re-enable these two includes once -->
<!-- the breached flags are populated upstream. -->
<!-- {{< include jira/sla-first-response-breach-rate.qmd >}} -->
<!-- {{< include jira/sla-time-to-done-breach-rate.qmd >}} -->
